torch.float:\n1264\t        assert triton.cdiv(N, block_n) == Bs.shape[0]\n1265\t        assert triton.cdiv(K, block_k) == Bs.shape[1]\n1266\t    elif Bs.dtype == torch.int:\n1267\t        assert N == 0)\n1345\t\n1346\t    def grid(META):\n1347\t        return (\n1348\t            triton.cdiv(M, META[\"BLOCK_SIZE_M\"]) * triton.cdiv(N, META[\"BLOCK_SIZE_N\"]),\n1349\t        )\n1350\t\n1351\t format.\n1737\t\n1738\tArgs:\n1739\t    input (torch.Tensor): Input tensor to be quantized\n1740\t    scale (Optional[torch.Tensor]): Pre-computed scaling factor for static quantization.\n1741\t       - scale_tensor: The scaling factors used for quantization\n1753\t\n1754\tRaises:\n1755\t    AssertionError: If input is not 2D or if static scale's numel != 1\n1756\t\"\"\"\n1757\tif _is_hip:\n1758\t\n1759\t    def _native_dynamic_per_token_quant_fp8(output, input, scale):\n1760\t        \"\"\"Native PyTorch fallback for dynamic per-token FP8 quantization when vLLM is unavailable.\"\"\"\n1761\t        M, N = input.shape\n1762\t        eps = 1e-12\n1763\t        # Compute per-token scale\n1764\t        absmax = input.abs().max(dim=1, keepdim=True).values\n1765\t        absmax = torch.clamp(absmax, min=eps)\n1766\t        scale_val = absmax / fp8_max\n1767\t        scale.copy_(scale_val)\n1768\t        # Quantize\n1769\t        output_data = torch.clamp(input / scale_val, fp8_min, fp8_max).to(fp8_dtype)\n1770\t        output.copy_(output_data)\n1771\t\n1772\t    def _native_dynamic_per_tensor_quant_fp8(output, input, scale):\n1773\t        \"\"\"Native unavailable.\"\"\"\n1786\t        # Use tensor directly instead of .item() to avoid CPU-GPU sync\n1787\t        output_data = torch.clamp(input / scale, fp8_min, fp8_max).to(fp8_dtype)\n1788\t  \"\"\"\n176\t    assert x.dtype == torch.int8\n177\t    assert x.ndim == 2\n178\t    out_dim, in_dim = x.size()\n179\t    in_dim *= 2\n180\t    fp8_block_size = 128\n181\t    fp4_block_size = 32\n182\t    assert in_dim % fp8_block_size == 0 and out_dim % fp8_block_size == 0\n183\t    assert scale.size(0) == out_dim and scale.size(1) == in_dim // fp4_block_size\n184\t\n185\t    x = dim=-1).flatten(2)\n190\t\n191\t    # max_fp4 (6.0) * MAX_OFFSET must fit in e4m3fn (max 448)\n192\t    # 6.0 * 2^6 = 384 < 448; 6.0 * 2^7 = 768 > 448; so MAX_OFFSET_BITS = 6\n193\t    MAX_OFFSET_BITS = 6\n194\t\n195\t    bOut = out_dim // fp8_block_size\n196\t    bIn = in_dim // fp8_block_size\n197\t    # bOut, bIn, 128, 128\n198\t    x = x.view(bOut, fp8_block_size, bIn, fp8_block_size).transpose(1, 2)\n199\t    # bOut, bIn, 128*4\n200\t    scale = scale.float().view(bOut, fp8_block_size, bIn, -1).transpose(1, get_moe_weight_sizes(inter_dim, is_concat, is_packed, is_aiter_moe):\n562\t    \"\"\"\n563\t    Calculate dimensions for MoE weight tensors.\n564\t\n565\t    Args:\n566\t        inter_dim: Base intermediate dimension.\n567\t        is_concat: If True, fusions W1 (gate) and W3 (up) projections.\n568\t        is_packed: If True, uses 4-bit quantization (two FP4 elements per byte).\n569\t        is_aiter_moe: If True, applies Aiter-specific kernel padding alignment.\n570\t    \"\"\"\n571\t    # w2_down_dim is the packing rank, but w13_up_dim not (of matrix to matmul)\n572\t    w13_up_dim = 2 * inter_dim if is_concat else inter_dim\n573\t    w2_down_dim = inter_dim // 2 if is_packed else inter_dim\n574\t\n575\t    if is_aiter_moe:\n576\t        padding_size = get_moe_padding_size(True)\n577\t        align_aiter = lambda n: ((n + padding_size - 1) // padding_size) * padding_size\n578\t        is_padded = (w2_down_dim % padding_size) > 0\n579\t        if is_padded:\n580\t            # w2_down_dim, padding & aligned, unit: parameter dtype\n581\t            w2_down_dim = align_aiter(w2_down_dim)\n582\t        # up proj + gate fusion : 2x\n583\t        if is_concat:\n584\t            w13_up_dim = w2_down_dim * 2\n585\t        # packed\n586\t        if hasattr(torch, \"float4_e2m1fn_x2\") and is_packed:\n587\t            # w13_up_dim (row rank of matmul matrix) is not packing dim, *2 to recover\n588\t            w13_up_dim *= 2\n589\t\n590\t    return (w13_up_dim, w2_down_dim, False if not is_aiter_moe else data type for DeepEP.\n\n    - BF16: dispatch hidden states in bf16\n    - FP8: dispatch hidden states in fp8\n    - INT8: dispatch hidden states in int8\n    - NVFP4: dispatch hidden states in nvfp4\n    \"\"\"\n\n    BF16 = \"bf16\"\n    FP8 = \"fp8\"\n    INT8 = \"int8\"\n    NVFP4 = \"nvfp4\"\n\n\ndef get_deepep_output_dtype(self) -> DeepEPOutputDtype:\n    \"\"\"\n    Automatically choose the dispatch output dtype for DeepEP.\n\n    The decision follows several checks in priority order:\n    0. Parse server argument.\n    1. Parse deprecated environment variables.\n    2. If quant_config contains input_global_scale → NVFP4 path.\n    3. Parse quant config\n    4. If flashinfer_cutedsl or is_cutlass backend is active → BF16 (it quantizes hidden_states internally).\n    5. Otherwise default for NPU → BF16 (the default for NPU).\n    6. Otherwise → FP8 (the default for most models like DeepSeek-V3).\n    \"\"\"\n\n    # 0. Parse server argument.\n    server_args = get_global_server_args()\n    if server_args and server_args.deepep_dispatcher_output_dtype != \"auto\":\n        return DeepEPOutputDtype(server_args.deepep_dispatcher_output_dtype)\n\n    # 1. Parse deprecated environment variables.\n    if envs.SGLANG_DEEPEP_BF16_DISPATCH.get():\n        logger.warning_once(\n            \"Warning: The env variable SGLANG_DEEPEP_BF16_DISPATCH deprecated \"\n            \"and will be removed in future releases. Please use a new \"\n            \"`--deepep-dispatcher-output-dtype bf16` argument instead.\"\n        )\n        return DeepEPOutputDtype.BF16\n\n    # 2. NVFP4 is detected inside dispatch_a / _dispatch_core via quant_config; no need to infer here.\n    if self.quant_config is not None:\n        input_global_scale = self.quant_config.get(\"input_global_scale\", None)\n        if input_global_scale is not None:\n            return DeepEPOutputDtype.NVFP4\n\n        # 3. Parse quant config to determine the output dtype of dispatcher\n        dispatcher_output_dtype = self.quant_config.get(\"dispatcher_output_dtype\", None)\n        if dispatcher_output_dtype is not None:\n            return DeepEPOutputDtype(dispatcher_output_dtype)\n\n    # 4. flashinfer_cutedsl and is_cutlass expects BF16 dispatch\n    if (\n        get_moe_runner_backend().is_flashinfer_cutedsl()\n        or get_moe_runner_backend().is_cutlass()\n    ):\n        return DeepEPOutputDtype.BF16\n\n    # 5. Default on NPU → BF16\n    if _is_npu:\n        return DeepEPOutputDtype.BF16\n\n    # 6. Default → FP8\n    return DeepEPOutputDtype.FP8\n\n\nMOE_A2A_BACKEND: Optional[MoeA2ABackend] = None\nMOE_RUNNER_BACKEND: Optional[MoeRunnerBackend] = None\nSPECULATIVE_MOE_RUNNER_BACKEND: fuse, replace, or absorb it.\n\n    Skip reasons, in order:\n      - ``should_allreduce_fusion``: LayerCommunicator will fuse the all-reduce\n        with the next layer's residual all-reduce.\n      - ``use_reduce_scatter``: LayerCommunicator's post-attention scatter will\n        do reduce-scatter, which would double-reduce on top of an all-reduce.\n      - ``should_use_dp_reduce_scatterv()``: Not relevant to the EP all-reduce.\n      - ``get_moe_a2a_backend().is_flashinfer()``: the flashinfer A2A\n        dispatcher's ``MoeAlltoAll.combine`` already alltoall-reduces partial\n        MoE outputs back to the source rank, so any further EP/TP all-reduce\n        would double-count and overflow BF16. Mirrors TRTLLM's\n        ``not enable_alltoall`` gate\n        (``tensorrt_llm/_torch/modules/fused_moe/interface.py:879``).\n\n    The first two args are layer-context flags from ``LayerCommunicator`` and\n    default to ``False`` for models that don't use it. Default: Softmax -> TopK\n    Default = (0,)\n    # Renormalize: TopK -> Softmax\n    Renormalize = (1,)\n    # DeepSeekV3: Sigmoid -> RoutingBiasAdd -> Top2 in group -> Top4 groups -> Top8 experts from the Top4 groups\n    DeepSeekV3 = (2,)\n    # MiniMax2\n    MiniMax2 = (7,)\n    # Sigmoid: Sigmoid -> TopK (no renormalize)\n    Sigmoid = (8,)\n    # Unspecified\n    Unspecified = 9\n\n\nAITER_PADDING_SIZE = 128\nTRITON_PADDING_SIZE = 128\n\n\n# Unit of padding - context dependent\ndef get_moe_padding_size(is_aiter_moe):\n    if is_aiter_moe:\n        return AITER_PADDING_SIZE\n    else:\n        return (\n            TRITON_PADDING_SIZE\n            if bool(int(os.getenv(\"SGLANG_MOE_PADDING\", \"0\")))\n            else 0\n        )\n\n\ndef get_moe_weight_sizes(inter_dim, is_concat, is_packed, is_aiter_moe):\n    \"\"\"\n    Calculate dimensions for MoE weight tensors.\n\n    Args:\n        inter_dim: Base intermediate dimension.\n        is_concat: If True, fusions W1 (gate) and W3 (up) projections.\n        is_packed: If True, uses 4-bit quantization (two FP4 elements per byte).\n        is_aiter_moe: If True, applies Aiter-specific kernel padding alignment.\n    \"\"\"\n    # w2_down_dim is the packing rank, but w13_up_dim not (of matrix to matmul)\n    w13_up_dim = 2 * inter_dim if is_concat else inter_dim\n    w2_down_dim = inter_dim // 2 if is_packed else inter_dim\n\n    if is_aiter_moe:\n        padding_size = get_moe_padding_size(True)\n        align_aiter = lambda n: ((n + padding_size - 1) // padding_size) * padding_size\n        is_padded = (w2_down_dim % padding_size) > 0\n        if is_padded:\n            # Mirrors the original MoriEPMoE.run_moe_core\n258\t    decision tree.\"\"\"\n259\t    is_fp8_quant = weight_quant in (\n260\t        AiterQuantType.PER_128X128,\n261\t        AiterQuantType.PER_TOKEN,\n262\t    )\n263\t    is_w4a4 = weight_quant == AiterQuantType.PER_1X32\n264\t    is_fp4_dispatch = dispatch_a1_dtype == torch.float4_e2m1fn_x2\n265\t    has_dispatch_scale = dispatch_scale is not None\n266\t\n267\t    if is_w4a4:\n268\t        # W4A4 weights always run as per_1x32; FP8 dispatch is upscaled to BF16\n269\t        # before this point so dispatch_scale won't conflict.\n270\t        return AiterQuantType.PER_1X32\n271\t    if is_fp8_quant:\n272\t        return weight_quant\n273\t    # BF16 weights: lift to the dispatch-side quant type when scales are AiterQuantType.PER_128X128\n278\t    return AiterQuantType.NONE\n279\t\n280\t\n281\tdef _pre_permute_deepep_to_aiter(\n282\t    dispatch_output: Union[\n283\t        DeepEPNormalDispatchOutput,\n284\t MoeRunnerConfig,\n290\t    running_state: dict,\n291\t) -> AiterRunnerInput:\n292\t    is_mori = _is_mori_dispatch_output(dispatch_output)\n293\t\n294\t    hidden_states = dispatch_output.hidden_states\n295\t    topk_ids = dispatch_output.topk_ids.to(torch.int32)\n296\t    topk_weights = dispatch_output.topk_weights.to(torch.float32)\n297\t    a1_scale:   if is_mori:\n303\t        from sglang.srt.layers.moe.rocm_moe_utils import upscale, upscale_mxfp4\n304\t\n305\t        a1_scale = dispatch_output.hidden_states_scale\n306\t        num_local_tokens = dispatch_output.num_recv_tokens_per_expert\n307\t        output_dtype = dispatch_output.out_dtype\n308\t\n309\t        # Truncate dispatch tensors to the configured cap; mori combine only\n310\t        # reads [0, totalRecvTokenNum), so the truncated result needs no\n311\t        # padding back.\n312\t        mori_max = get_int_env_var(\"SGLANG_MORI_MOE_MAX_INPUT_TOKENS\", 0)\n313\t        if mori_max > 0:\n314\t            hidden_states = hidden_states[:mori_max]\n315\t            if a1_scale is not None:\n316\t                a1_scale = a1_scale[:mori_max]\n317\t            topk_ids = weight/activation dtype pair.\n322\t        weight_quant = quant_info.quant_type\n323\t        is_fp8_quant = weight_quant in (\n324\t            AiterQuantType.PER_128X128,\n325\t            AiterQuantType.PER_TOKEN,\n326\t        )\n327\t        is_w4a4 = weight_quant == AiterQuantType.PER_1X32\n328\t        is_fp4_dispatch = hidden_states.dtype == torch.float4_e2m1fn_x2\n329\t\n330\t        # AITER fused_moe Clamped-SwiGLU is dispatched with\n331\t        # gate_mode=INTERLEAVE, for which AITER picks a bf16/fp8 `q_dtype_a`\n332\t        # Refer to https://github.com/ROCm/aiter/blob/a2617c366dc7271a1662ecda2023d19f6ccefcec/aiter/fused_moe.py#L406-L412\n333\t        swiglu_interleave = quant_info.swiglu_limit > 0 and get_bool_env_var(\n334\t            \"SGLANG_USE_AITER_MOE_GU_ITLV\", \"true\"\n335\t        )\n336\t\n337\t        if is_w4a4 and a1_scale is not None and not is_fp4_dispatch:\n338\t            # W4A4 weights with FP8 dispatch: output_dtype\n342\t            )\n343\t            a1_scale = None\n344\t        elif is_w4a4 and is_fp4_dispatch and a1_scale is not None and swiglu_interleave:\n345\t            # W4A4 weights + FP4        )\n368\t    else:\n369\t        # DeepEP marks invalid topk slots with idx == -1; AITER cannot accept\n370\t        # negative ids, so reroute them to the sink slot at index\n371\t        #       param: torch.nn.Parameter,\n416\t        weight_name: str,\n417\t    ) -> None:\n418\t        \"\"\"Restore canonical BF16 MoE load shapes before hot weight copy.\n419\t\n420\t        The flashinfer TRT-LLM BF16 postprocess reshapes expert weights into\n421\t        block layout. layer.intermediate_size_per_partition,\n440\t            )\n441\t\n442\t        if expected_shape is None or tuple(param.data.shape) == expected_shape:\n443\t            return\n444\t\n445\t )\n451\t\n452\t        param.data = param.data.reshape(expected_shape)\n453\t\n454\t    def _aiter_ck_moe_supported(self, layer) -> bool:\n455\t        # aiter CK fused-MoE requires                return build_lora_hooks(\n                    hidden_states,\n                    lora_info,\n                    topk_ids,\n                )\n            return None\n\n        #      runner_input = self.pre_permute_func(\n            dispatch_output, quant_info, self.config, running_state\n        )\n\n        hooks = _maybe_build_lora_hooks(runner_input)\n\n        runner_output = self.runner_core.run(\n            runner_input, quant_info, running_state, hooks=hooks\n        )\n        runner_format = self.runner_core.runner_backend.value\n (`/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/quantization/unquant.py`):\n\nOn AMD with `SGLANG_USE_AITER=1`, the unquantized linear path uses `aiter.tuned_gemm.tgemm.mm()` (line 202) -- the AITER tuned GEMM dispatcher which auto-selects the best GEMM kernel (hipBLASLt, Triton, CK).\n\n**Router GEMM** (`/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/rocm_linear_utils.py`):\n\nThe DeepSeek-V3 router GEMM uses `tgemm.mm()` from AITER's tuned_gemm dispatcher (line 14), which automatically selects between hipBLASLt and other backends.\n\n**Triton Kernel Tuning Configs** (`/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/quantization/configs/`):\n\nThere are pre-tuned JSON config files for different GPU architectures, including AMD:\n- `AMD_Instinct_MI300X`\n- `AMD_Instinct_MI300X_VF`\n- `AMD_Instinct_MI325X`\n- `AMD_Radeon_Graphics`\n\nThese configs map batch sizes (M) to Triton meta-parameters (BLOCK_SIZE_M/N/K, GROUP_SIZE_M, num_warps, num_stages, waves_per_eu). For example, an MI300X config for N=1536,K=7168 uses BLOCK_SIZE_K=128, GROUP_SIZE_M=4, num_warps=4, num_stages=2.\n\n**AMD-specific unrolled kernel** (fp8_kernel.py, line 1214):\n\nOn HIP, `select_w8a8_block_fp8_matmul_kernel` picks between the standard `_w8a8_block_fp8_matmul` and a manually 4x-unrolled variant `_w8a8_block_fp8_matmul_unrolledx4`. The unrolled variant is used when the number of workgroups is less than or equal to the device's compute unit count (line 1220-1224). This trades instruction scheduling overhead for register pressure when the grid is small.\n\n### 2. Kernel Fusion Opportunities\n\n**Environment flags controlling fusion** (from `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/environ.py`):\n\nAMD/ROCm-specific fusion flags:\n- `SGLANG_AITER_FUSE_RMSNORM_PAD` (line 499): Fuses `residual_add + RMSNorm + zero-pad` for MoE input padding\n- `SGLANG_ROCM_FUSED_DECODE_MLA` (line 510): Fused MLA decode on ROCm\n- `SGLANG_ROCM_USE_MULTI_STREAM` (line 515): Dual-stream MoE (shared vs routed experts), requires `GPU_MAX_HW_QUEUES>=5`\n\nGeneral fusion flags that apply on AMD (DeepSeek-V4 path):\n- `SGLANG_OPT_USE_FUSED_QK_NORM_ROPE` (line 817): Fused QK norm + RoPE\n- `SGLANG_OPT_USE_FUSED_CLAMP_ACT_MUL` (line 818): Fused clamp + activation + multiply\n- `SGLANG_OPT_USE_FUSED_STORE_CACHE` (line 907): Fused KV cache store\n- `SGLANG_OPT_FUSE_WQA_WKV` (line 903): Fused weight QA/KV projections\n- `SGLANG_OPT_SWIGLU_CLAMP_FUSION` (line 904): SwiGLU + clamp fusion\n- `SGLANG_OPT_USE_FUSED_COMPRESS` / `SGLANG_OPT_USE_FUSED_COMPRESS_TRITON` (lines 815-816)\n- `SGLANG_ENABLE_NVFP4_GEMM_SWIGLU_FUSION` (line 819): GEMM + SwiGLU fusion\n- `SGLANG_DSA_FUSE_TOPK` (line 625): Fused topk for DSA attention\n\n**Fused operations in jit_kernel/** directory:\n- `fused_a_gemm.py`: Fused QKV-A GEMM for DeepSeek-V3 (dispatches to AOT, JIT, or CuTe DSL backends)\n- `fused_store_index_cache.py`: Fused KV store + index cache\n- `fused_metadata_copy.py`: Fused metadata copy\n- `fused_eh_norm.py`: Fused element-wise + head norm\n- `fused_qknorm_rope.py`: Fused QK normalization + RoPE\n- `moe_fused_gate.py`: Fused MoE gate computation\n- `moe_finalize_fuse_shared.py`: Fused MoE finalization with shared experts\n\n**AMD-specific fused kernels** (`/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/rocm_linear_utils.py`):\n- `fused_qk_rope_cat_and_cache_mla` from aiter: Fuses QK RoPE + cat + MLA cache\n- `fused_qk_rope_cat` from aiter: Fuses QK RoPE + concatenation\n\n**MXFP8 MoE on gfx95** (`/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/moe/moe_runner/triton_utils/mxfp8_moe_amd_gfx95.py`):\n\nThis is a specialized fused MoE kernel for AMD CDNA4 (gfx950). It fuses:\n- Token sorting by expert (via `moe_align_block_size`)\n- GEMM1 with token-id indirection (MXFP8 quant happens once, not top_k times)\n- SwiGLU activation (fused fp32 Triton kernel)\n- GEMM2 with top-k weight application (no atomics)\nAll using `tl.dot_scaled` for native microscaling on CDNA4.\n\n### 3. global_config.py\n\n`/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/global_config.py` is a **minimal legacy file** (29 lines), marked for deprecation (line 3: \"FIXME: deprecate this file\"). It contains:\n\n- `verbosity`: Logging level (0 = silent, 2 = final text after every run)\n- `default_backend`: Default language backend\n- `skip_special_tokens_in_output`: Output tokenization config\n- `spaces_between_special_tokens_in_out`: Output tokenization config\n- `enable_precache_with_tracing`: Language frontend optimization\n- `enable_parallel_encoding`: Language frontend optimization\n\n**The real performance configuration lives in** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/environ.py` (the `Envs` class, ~984 lines). This contains all tunable environment variables exposed as `SGLANG_*` flags.\n\n### 4. Environment Variables Affecting AMD Performance\n\nThe key AMD/ROCm performance env vars are in `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/environ.py`:\n\n**Core AMD flags:**\n- `SGLANG_USE_AITER` (line 477): Master switch for AITER kernel library (AMD-specific). Gates usage of CK GEMMs, optimized RMSNorm, attention, etc.\n- `SGLANG_USE_AITER_AG` (line 478): AITER all-gather integration (default: True)\n- `SGLANG_USE_AITER_UNIFIED_ATTN` (line 484): AITER unified attention path\n- `SGLANG_USE_AITER_MOE_GU_ITLV` (line 489): Gate/up tile layout for AITER MoE (interleave vs separated)\n- `SGLANG_AITER_KV_CACHE_LAYOUT` (line 509): KV cache physical layout: \"nhd\" (default) or \"vectorized_5d\" (SHUFFLE layout for CK/gluon kernels)\n- `SGLANG_AITER_FUSE_RMSNORM_PAD` (line 499): Fuse RMSNorm + pad in MoE path\n- `SGLANG_ROCM_FUSED_DECODE_MLA` (line 510): Fused MLA decode\n- `SGLANG_ROCM_DISABLE_LINEARQUANT` (line 511): Disable linear quantization on ROCm (forces unquantized QKV/RowParallel)\n- `SGLANG_ROCM_USE_MULTI_STREAM` (line 515): Dual-stream MoE\n- `SGLANG_DP_USE_REDUCE_SCATTER` (line 483): Default ON for ROCm, uses aiter symmetric-memory reduce_scatter\n- `SGLANG_USE_1STAGE_ALLREDUCE` (line 666): 1-stage all-reduce (deterministic, fixed accumulation order) on AMD\n- `SGLANG_USE_AITER_FP8_PER_TOKEN` (line 933): AITER FP8 per-token quantization\n- `SGLANG_OPT_USE_AITER_SILU_MUL` (line 814): Use AITER SiLU activation\n- `SGLANG_OPT_USE_AITER_MHC_PRE` / `SGLANG_OPT_USE_AITER_MHC_POST` (lines 812-813): AITER multi-head cache pre/post\n- `SGLANG_OPT_USE_AITER_INDEXER` (line 838): AITER DSV4 indexer\n\n**ROCm all-reduce / communication:**\n- `ROCM_QUICK_REDUCE_CAST_BF16_TO_FP16` (external env, read in quick_all_reduce.py)\n- `ROCM_QUICK_REDUCE_QUANTIZATION` (external env: \"NONE\", FP8, etc.)\n- `ROCM_QUICK_REDUCE_MAX_SIZE_BYTES_MB` (external env)\n\n**AITER attention backend** (`/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/attention/aiter_backend.py`):\n- `SGLANG_AITER_MLA_PERSIST` (line 80): Use persistent MLA kernel design for FP8 KV cache\n- `SGLANG_AITER_FP8_PREFILL_ATTN` (line 83): FP8 prefill attention (gfx95 only)\n\n### 5. Weight Quantization / Processing During Model Loading\n\n**FP8 Weight Processing** (`/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/quantization/fp8.py`):\n\nThe `Fp8LinearMethod.process_weights_after_loading` (line 722) performs:\n- `normalize_e4m3fn_to_e4m3fnuz()` on AMD MI300 (gfx94): Converts FP8 e4m3fn weights to e4m3fnuz format (the native AMD format), handling the NaN bit-pattern difference and doubling scales (lines 212-233 of fp8_utils.py)\n- `shuffle_weight()` (from `aiter.ops.shuffle`): Reorders weight tensor to the (16,16) block layout expected by CK GEMM kernels when AITER or INT4 is active (line 639, 743, 788)\n- `shuffle_scale()`: Reorders scale tensors to match CK preshuffle layout for MoE weights (line 1322)\n- `prepare_fp8_layer_for_marlin()`: Converts FP8 weights to Marlin-optimized format when `SGLANG_FORCE_FP8_MARLIN=1`\n\n**MXFP8 weight handling** (`/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/quantization/mxfp8_amd_gfx95.py`):\n\nFor CDNA4 (gfx950), MXFP8 weights are stored as FP8 E4M3 values with UE8M0 (uint8) block scales. No load-time reformat is needed -- the `tl.dot_scaled` kernel consumes them directly.\n\n**Quant config disabling on HIP** (`/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/linear.py`, line 52):\n\nWhen `SGLANG_ROCM_DISABLE_LINEARQUANT=1`, QKVParallelLinear and RowParallelLinear set `quant_config = None`, forcing unquantized computation. On all other platforms, `fp8_dtype = torch.float8_e4m3fn`.\n\n### 6. is_hip / ROCm Detection and Branching in Performance-Critical Paths\n\nThe `is_hip()` check (from `sglang.srt.utils`, resolves to `torch.version.hip is not None`) is cached at module import time as `_is_hip` in every performance-critical module. Environment Variables Affecting AMD Performance\n\nThe key AMD/ROCm performance env vars are in `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/environ.py`:\n\n**Core AMD flags:**\n- `SGLANG_USE_AITER` (line 477): Master switch for AITER kernel library (AMD-specific). Gates usage of CK GEMMs, optimized RMSNorm, attention, etc.\n- `SGLANG_USE_AITER_AG` (line 478): AITER all-gather integration (default: True)\n- `SGLANG_USE_AITER_UNIFIED_ATTN` (line 484): AITER unified attention path\n- `SGLANG_USE_AITER_MOE_GU_ITLV` (line 489): Gate/up tile layout for AITER MoE (interleave vs separated)\n- `SGLANG_AITER_KV_CACHE_LAYOUT` (line 509): KV cache physical layout: \"nhd\" (default) or \"vectorized_5d\" (SHUFFLE layout for CK/gluon kernels)\n- `SGLANG_AITER_FUSE_RMSNORM_PAD` (line 499): Fuse RMSNorm + pad in MoE path\n- `SGLANG_ROCM_FUSED_DECODE_MLA` (line 510): Fused MLA decode\n- `SGLANG_ROCM_DISABLE_LINEARQUANT` (line 511): Disable linear quantization on ROCm (forces unquantized QKV/RowParallel)\n- `SGLANG_ROCM_USE_MULTI_STREAM` (line 515): Dual-stream MoE\n- `SGLANG_DP_USE_REDUCE_SCATTER` (line 483): Default ON for ROCm, uses aiter symmetric-memory reduce_scatter\n- `SGLANG_USE_1STAGE_ALLREDUCE` (line 666): 1-stage all-reduce (deterministic, fixed accumulation order) on AMD\n- `SGLANG_USE_AITER_FP8_PER_TOKEN` (line 933): AITER FP8 per-token quantization\n- `SGLANG_OPT_USE_AITER_SILU_MUL` (line 814): Use AITER SiLU activation\n- `SGLANG_OPT_USE_AITER_MHC_PRE` / `SGLANG_OPT_USE_AITER_MHC_POST` (lines 812-813): AITER multi-head cache pre/post\n- `SGLANG_OPT_USE_AITER_INDEXER` (line 838): AITER DSV4 indexer\n\n**ROCm all-reduce / communication:**\n- `ROCM_QUICK_REDUCE_CAST_BF16_TO_FP16` (external env, read in quick_all_reduce.py)\n- `ROCM_QUICK_REDUCE_QUANTIZATION` (external env: \"NONE\", FP8, etc.)\n- `ROCM_QUICK_REDUCE_MAX_SIZE_BYTES_MB` (external env)\n\n**AITER attention backend** (`/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/attention/aiter_backend.py`):\n- `SGLANG_AITER_MLA_PERSIST` (line 80): Use persistent MLA kernel design for FP8 KV cache\n- `SGLANG_AITER_FP8_PREFILL_ATTN` (line 83): FP8 prefill attention (gfx95 only)\n\n### 5. Weight Quantization / Processing During Model Loading\n\n**FP8 Weight Processing** (`/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/quantization/fp8.py`):\n\nThe `Fp8LinearMethod.process_weights_after_loading` (line 722) performs:\n- `normalize_e4m3fn_to_e4m3fnuz()` on AMD MI300 (gfx94): Converts FP8 e4m3fn weights to e4m3fnuz format (the native AMD format), handling the NaN bit-pattern difference and doubling scales (lines 212-233 of fp8_utils.py)\n- `shuffle_weight()` (from `aiter.ops.shuffle`): Reorders weight tensor to the (16,16) block layout expected by CK GEMM kernels when AITER or INT4 is active (line 639, 743, 788)\n- `shuffle_scale()`: Reorders scale tensors to match CK preshuffle layout for MoE weights (line 1322)\n- `prepare_fp8_layer_for_marlin()`: Converts FP8 weights to Marlin-optimized format when `SGLANG_FORCE_FP8_MARLIN=1`\n\n**MXFP8 weight handling** (`/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/quantization/mxfp8_amd_gfx95.py`):\n\nFor CDNA4 (gfx950), MXFP8 weights are stored as FP8 E4M3 values with UE8M0 (uint8) block scales. No load-time reformat is needed -- the `tl.dot_scaled` kernel consumes them directly.\n\n**Quant config disabling on HIP** (`/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/linear.py`, line 52):\n\nWhen `SGLANG_ROCM_DISABLE_LINEARQUANT=1`, QKVParallelLinear and RowParallelLinear set `quant_config = None`, forcing unquantized computation. This is gated by `_disable_hip_linear_quant = _is_hip and get_bool_env_var(\"SGLANG_ROCM_DISABLE_LINEARQUANT\")`.\n\n**FP8 dtype selection** (`/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/quantization/fp8_kernel.py`, line 123):\n\nOn MI300 (gfx94), `is_fp8_fnuz()` returns True, causing `fp8_dtype = torch.float8_e4m3fnuz` and `fp8_max = 224.0`. On all other platforms, `fp8_dtype = torch.float8_e4m3fn`.\n\n### 6. is_hip / ROCm Detection and Branching in Performance-Critical Paths\n\nThe `is_hip()` check (from `sglang.srt.utils`, resolves to `torch.version.hip is not None`) is cached at module import time as `_is_hip` in every performance-critical module. Here is a summary of the branching:\n\n**Activation layer** (`/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/activation.py`):\n- `_is_hip` (line 56): Imports `sgl_kernel.silu_and_mul`, `gelu_and_mul`, `gelu_quick`, `gelu_tanh_and_mul` from sgl-kernel (ROCm build)\n- `_use_aiter` (line 58): Optionally uses `aiter.silu_and_mul` when `SGLANG_OPT_USE_AITER_SILU_MUL` is set\n\n**LayerNorm** (`/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/layernorm.py`):\n- With AITER: Uses `aiter.rmsnorm2d_fwd`, `aiter.rmsnorm2d_fwd_with_add`, `aiter.layernorm2d_fwd` (lines 98-101)\n- Without AITER but with HIP: Falls back to `vllm._custom_ops.rms_norm` / `fused_add_rms_norm` (lines 104-108)\n- Without either: Uses native PyTorch fallback\n\n**FP8 quantization** (`/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/quantization/fp8_kernel.py`):\n- Line 81-99: On HIP with AITER, imports `aiter.dynamic_per_tensor_quant`, `dynamic_per_token_scaled_quant`, `static_per_tensor_quant`\n- Line 93-99: On HIP without AITER, tries vLLM fallback, then native PyTorch\n- Lines 1757-1896: The `scaled_fp8_quant` function has an entire HIP-specific branch with native PyTorch fallback implementations for per-token, per-tensor, and static quantization\n- Line 760-763: `per_token_group_quant_fp8` dispatches to `sglang_per_token_group_quant_fp8` on CUDA, falls back to `_per_token_group_quant_8bit_raw` (raw Triton) elsewhere\n\n**FP8 GEMM kernel selection** (fp8_kernel.py, lines 1214-1229):\n- On HIP, `select_w8a8_block_fp8_matmul_kernel` picks the unrolled kernel when the workgroup count is small relative to CU count\n\n**MoE** (`/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/moe/rocm_moe_utils.py`):\n- Provides AMD-specific fused MoE kernels via AITER's `asm_moe_tkw1` (assembly-level MoE for top-k=1 with FP8)\n- The `rocm_fused_experts_tkw1` function wraps this for per-channel FP8 with `apply_router_weight_on_input`\n\n**Model runner** (`/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/model_executor/model_runner.py`):\n- Line 2454: KV cache dtype set to `fp8_dtype` (e4m3fnuz on MI300) when HIP\n- Lines 2461, 2466: Uses natively supported FP8 format on HIP\n\n**Attention** (`/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/attention/aiter_backend.py`):\n- Entire file is the AITER attention backend, using `aiter.flash_attn_varlen_func`, `mla_decode_fwd`, `mla_prefill_fwd`, `paged_attention_ragged`, `unified_attention`, and the MLA persistent kernel design\n\n**Quick All-Reduce** (`/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/distributed/device_communicators/quick_all_reduce.py`):\n- Supports only `gfx94` and `gfx95` architectures (MI300/MI350 series, line 34)\n- Reads `ROCM_QUICK_REDUCE_CAST_BF16_TO_FP16`, `ROCM_QUICK_REDUCE_QUANTIZATION`, `ROCM_QUICK_REDUCE_MAX_SIZE_BYTES_MB`\n\n**Overlap scheduler** (environ.py, line 341):\n- `SGLANG_ENABLE_WAR_BARRIER`: Force-enables the write-after-read barrier on non-CUDA (AMD/ROCm) Models with MHA Architecture (e.g: Llama, QWen)\n            1.1 We will turn on FA3 on hopper unless user use spec decode with topk > 1 or page_size > 1.\n            1.2 Use trtllm_mha for SM100/SM103 (Blackwell B200/GB200/B300) excluding spec with topk > 1.\n               Note: trtllm_mha does not support SM120, which will fall back to flashinfer.\n            1.3 In other cases, we will use flashinfer if available, otherwise use triton.\n        2. Models with MLA Architecture and using FA3\n            2.1 We will use FA3 backend on hopper.\n            2.2 We will use Flashinfer backend on blackwell.\n            2.3 Otherwise, we will use triton backend.\n        \"\"\"\n        # OOT platforms provide their own default attention backend.\n        if current_platform.is_out_of_tree():\n            return current_platform.get_default_attention_backend()\n\n        # Whisper requires flashinfer for cross-attention CUDA graph support.\n        if \"WhisperForConditionalGeneration\" in (\n            model_config.hf_config.architectures or []\n        ):\n            return \"flashinfer\"\n\n        if not use_mla_backend:\n            # MHA architecture\n            from sglang.srt.arg_groups.overrides import resolved_view\n\n            if is_hopper_with_cuda_12_3() and is_no_spec_infer_or_topk_one(\n                resolved_view(self)\n            ):\n                # Note: flashinfer 0.6.1 caused performance regression on Hopper attention kernel\n                # Before the kernel is fixed, we choose fa3 as the default backend on Hopper MHA\n                # ref: https://github.com/sgl-project/sglang/issues/17411\n                return \"fa3\"\n            elif (\n                is_sm100_supported()\n                and is_no_spec_infer_or_topk_one(resolved_view(self))\n                and (\n                    self.speculative_algorithm is None\n                    or self.speculative_eagle_topk is not None\n                )\n            ):\n                return \"trtllm_mha\"\n            elif is_hip():\n                return \"aiter\"\n            elif is_mps():\n                return \"torch_native\"\n            else:\n                # FlashInfer does not support attention sinks.\n                if is_flashinfer_available() and not model_config.has_attention_sinks:\n                    return \"flashinfer\"\n                return \"triton\"\n        else:\n            # MLA architecture\n            if is_hopper_with_cuda_12_3():\n                return \"fa3\"\n            elif is_sm100_supported():\n                return \"flashinfer\"\n            elif is_hip():\n                head_num = model_config.get_num_kv_heads(self.tp_size)\n                # TODO current aiter only support head number 16 or 128 head number\n                if head_num == 128 or head_num == 16:\n                    return \"aiter\"\n                else:\n                    return \"triton\"\n            elif is_mps():\n                return \"torch_native\"\n            else:\n                return \"triton\"\n\n    def _handle_attention_backend_compatibility(self):\n        model_config = self.get_model_config()\n\n        # StandardCombineInput(\n        hidden_states=output,\n    )\n\n\n@register_pre_permute(\"standard\", \"triton\")\ndef pre_permute_standard_to_triton(\n    dispatch_output: StandardDispatchOutput,\n    quant_info: TritonMoeQuantInfo,\n    runner_config: MoeRunnerConfig,\n    running_state: dict,\n) -> TritonRunnerInput:\n\n    # Registered fallback for format-conversion tests and examples.\n\n down_config,\n        down_moe_use_tma,\n        sorted_token_ids,\n        expert_ids,\n        num_tokens_post_padded,\n    ) = _prepare_fused_moe_run(\n        hidden_states,\n        quant_info.w13_weight,\n        quant_info.w2_weight,\n        topk_output.topk_ids,\n        use_fp8_w8a8=quant_info.use_fp8_w8a8,\n        use_int8_w8a8=quant_info.use_int8_w8a8,\n        use_int8_w8a16=quant_info.use_int8_w8a16,\n        use_int4_w4a16=quant_info.use_int4_w4a16,\n        per_channel_quant=quant_info.per_channel_quant,\n        block_shape=quant_info.block_shape,\n    )\n\n    running_state[\"config\"] = config\n    running_state[\"down_config\"] = down_config\n    running_state[\"down_moe_use_tma\"] = down_moe_use_tma\n\n    return TritonRunnerInput(\n Let me compile the findings.\n\n---\n\nHere is a thorough report on how the attention backend is selected in sglang, with particular focus on AMD/ROCm paths.\n\n## 1. Attention Backend Selection Logic\n\n### Central Registry\n\nThe backend selection is built around a **registry pattern** defined in `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/attention/attention_registry.py`. Each backend is registered with a decorator:\n\n```python\nATTENTION_BACKENDS = {}\n\ndef register_attention_backend(name):\n    def decorator(fn):\n        ATTENTION_BACKENDS[name] = fn\n        return fn\n    return decorator\n```\n\nThe registered backends include: `flashinfer`, `trtllm_mla`, `tokenspeed_mla`, `cutedsl_mla`, `aiter`, `wave`, `ascend`, `dsa` (formerly `nsa`), `dsv4`, `triton`, `torch_native`, `flex_attention`, `flashmla`, `fa3`, `fa4`, `cutlass_mla`, `trtllm_mha`, `intel_amx`, `dual_chunk_flash_attn`, `intel_xpu`.\n\n### The Complete Choice List\n\nDefined in `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/server_args.py` (line 169):\n\n```python\nATTENTION_BACKEND_CHOICES = [\n    # Common\n    \"triton\", \"torch_native\", \"flex_attention\", \"dsa\", \"nsa\", \"dsv4\", \"compressed\",\n    # NVIDIA specific\n    \"cutlass_mla\", \"fa3\", \"fa4\", \"flashinfer\", \"flashmla\", \"trtllm_mla\",\n    \"cutedsl_mla\", \"tokenspeed_mla\", \"trtllm_mha\", \"dual_chunk_flash_attn\",\n    # AMD specific\n    \"aiter\", \"wave\",\n    # Other platforms\n    \"intel_amx\", \"ascend\", \"intel_xpu\",\n]\n```\n\n### User Configuration\n\nUsers can configure backends via three CLI arguments (defined in `server_args.py`, lines 1277-1300):\n- `--attention-backend` -- sets backend for both prefill and decode\n- `--prefill-attention-backend` -- overrides for prefill only\n- `--decode-attention-backend` -- overrides for decode only\n- `--speculative-draft-attention-backend` (line 1644) -- for speculative decoding draft model\n- `--mm-attention-backend` -- multimodal (vision encoder) attention backend\n\n### Auto-Selection: `_get_default_attn_backend`\n\nWhen no backend is explicitly set, the auto-selection happens in `_get_default_attn_backend` (lines 4310-4378 in `server_args.py`). The logic is split by architecture:\n\n**For MHA (Multi-Head Attention) models (e.g., Llama, Qwen):**\n1. Hopper (SM90) with CUDA 12.3 and compatible speculative config -> `\"fa3\"` (FlashAttention v3)\n2. Blackwell SM100 with compatible spec config -> `\"trtllm_mha\"`\n3. **AMD/ROCm (`is_hip()`)** -> `\"aiter\"`\n4. MPS (Apple Silicon) -> `\"torch_native\"`\n5. Otherwise, if flashinfer is available and no attention sinks -> `\"flashinfer\"`, else -> `\"triton\"`\n\n**For MLA (Multi-Latent Attention) models (e.g., DeepSeek V3):**\n1. Hopper -> `\"fa3\"`\n2. Blackwell SM100 -> `\"flashinfer\"`\n3. **AMD/ROCm (`is_hip()`)** -> checks head count:\n   - If `head_num == 128 or head_num == 16` -> `\"aiter\"`\n   - Otherwise -> `\"triton\"`\n4. MPS -> `\"torch_native\"`\n5. Otherwise -> `\"triton\"`\n\n### Resolution Pipeline\n\nThe resolution flows through a pipeline in `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/arg_groups/overrides.py`. Key passes (called from `_handle_attention_backend_compatibility` at line 4380 in `server_args.py`):\n\n1. `_attention_backend_default` (overrides.py line 1503) -- fills in the default backend via `_get_default_attn_backend` if none was specified\n2. `_attention_backend_fa3_fp8_fallback` (line 1664) -- falls back from fa3 to triton for fp8_e5m2\n3. `_attention_backend_platform_fallbacks` (line 1697) -- Intel AMX/XPU fallbacks\n4. `_attention_backend_dual_chunk` (line 1738) -- dual chunk attention auto-enable\n\n### Instantiation in ModelRunner\n\nThe actual instantiation happens in `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/model_executor/model_runner.py`:\n- `init_attention_backend()` (line 2495) calls `_get_attention_backend()` (line 2516)\n- `_get_attention_backend()` resolves prefill/decode backends; if they differ, creates a `HybridAttnBackend` wrapping both\n- `_get_attention_backend_from_str()` (line 2569) looks up the string in `ATTENTION_BACKENDS` registry and calls the factory, then wraps the result through `attn_backend_wrapper()` for hybrid linear/mamba model support\n\n---\n\n## 2. AMD/ROCm-Specific Attention Paths\n\n### `aiter` Backend (Primary AMD Backend)\n\n**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/attention/aiter_backend.py`\n\nThis is the default for AMD GPUs. It imports from the `aiter` library (AMD's kernel library):\n- `flash_attn_varlen_func` -- for variable-length flash attention\n- `mla_decode_fwd`, `mla_prefill_fwd` -- MLA-specific kernels\n- `mla_prefill_ps_asm_fwd` -- persist-style MLA prefill\n- `paged_attention_ragged` -- paged attention for MHA decode\n- `unified_attention` -- from `aiter.ops.triton.gluon`\n\nThe aiter backend supports both MHA and MLA architectures (checked via `self.use_mla` at line 168).\n\n### `aiter` Utilities (SHUFFLE 5D Layout)\n\n**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/attention/aiter_utils.py`\n\nProvides SHUFFLE 5D KV-cache layout pathways (when `SGLANG_AITER_KV_CACHE_LAYOUT=vectorized_5d`), using:\n- `pa_decode_gluon` from `aiter.ops.triton.gluon`\n- `mha_batch_prefill_func` from aiter\n\n### `wave` Backend\n\n**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/attention/wave_backend.py`\n\nAn alternative AMD-specific backend using `wave_lang` kernels. Listed as AMD-specific in the choices.\n\n### `triton` Backend on AMD\n\n**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/attention/triton_backend.py`\n\nThe triton backend works on both NVIDIA and AMD. AMD-specific handling (lines 211-215):\n```python\nif _is_gfx942:\n    # gfx942's 304 CUs round up to 512 splits, doubling the persistent\n    # fp32 attn_logits buffer to ~4 GiB on Kimi-K2.6 and faulting in\n    # ROCm graph replay; pin to 256 to match validated gfx950 behavior.\n    self.max_kv_splits = min(self.max_kv_splits, 256)\n```\n\n### ROCm MLA Decode with RoPE (Triton Kernel)\n\n**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/attention/triton_ops/rocm_mla_decode_rope.py`\n\nA specialized triton kernel for MLA decoding with RoPE on ROCm, adapted from lightllm.\n\n### DeepSeek V4 HIP/Radix Backend\n\n**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/attention/deepseek_v4_backend_hip_radix.py`\n\nWhen `dsv4` backend is selected on AMD (via the registry at line 137-145 of `attention_registry.py`):\n```python\nelif _is_hip:\n    from sglang.srt.layers.attention.deepseek_v4_backend_hip_radix import (\n        DeepseekV4HipRadixBackend,\n    )\n    return DeepseekV4HipRadixBackend(runner)\n```\n\nThis is `class DeepseekV4HipRadixBackend(AttentionBackend, C4IndexerBackendMixin, CompressorBackendMixin)` at line 406, a full AMD-specific attention backend for DeepSeek V4 compressed attention.\n\n### DSV4 HIP Compressor\n\n**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/attention/dsv4/compress_hip.py`\n\nAMD-specific compressor for DSV4 attention, using triton kernels for pooling, normalization, and RoPE.\n\n### HIP Flash MLA\n\n**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/attention/hip_flash_mla.py`\n\nEntry point for flash MLA with KV-cache on HIP. On AMD, it routes to the `SGLANG_HACK_FLASHMLA_BACKEND` environment variable (default `\"tilelang\"`), supporting backends: `torch`, `tilelang`, `triton`.\n\n### Vision Attention on AMD\n\n**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/attention/vision.py`\n\nAMD multimodal attention auto-selection (lines 1094-1098):\n```python\nelif _is_hip:\n    if get_device_capability() >= (9, 4) and _use_aiter:\n        backend = \"aiter_attn\"\n    else:\n        backend = \"triton_attn\"\n```\n\nThe `VisionAiterAttention` class (line 656) is AMD-only and uses `aiter.flash_attn_varlen_func`.\n\n---\n\n## 3. AMD-specific handling (lines 211-215):\n```python\nif _is_gfx942:\n    # gfx942's 304 CUs round up to 512 splits, doubling the persistent\n    # fp32 attn_logits buffer to ~4 GiB on Kimi-K2.6 and faulting in\n    # ROCm graph replay; pin to 256 to match validated gfx950 behavior.\n    self.max_kv_splits = min(self.max_kv_splits, 256)\n```\n\n### ROCm MLA Decode with RoPE (Triton Kernel)\n\n**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/attention/triton_ops/rocm_mla_decode_rope.py`\n\nA specialized triton kernel for MLA decoding with RoPE on ROCm, adapted from lightllm.\n\n### DeepSeek V4 HIP/Radix Backend\n\n**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/attention/deepseek_v4_backend_hip_radix.py`\n\nWhen `dsv4` backend is selected on AMD (via the registry at line 137-145 of `attention_registry.py`):\n```python\nelif _is_hip:\n    from sglang.srt.layers.attention.deepseek_v4_backend_hip_radix import (\n        DeepseekV4HipRadixBackend,\n    )\n    return DeepseekV4HipRadixBackend(runner)\n```\n\nThis is `class DeepseekV4HipRadixBackend(AttentionBackend, C4IndexerBackendMixin, CompressorBackendMixin)` at line 406, a full AMD-specific attention backend for DeepSeek V4 compressed attention.\n\n### DSV4 HIP Compressor\n\n**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/attention/dsv4/compress_hip.py`\n\nAMD-specific compressor for DSV4 attention, using triton kernels for pooling, normalization, and RoPE.\n\n### HIP Flash MLA\n\n**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/attention/hip_flash_mla.py`\n\nEntry point for flash MLA with KV-cache on HIP. MoE Runner Selection Logic\n\n### Available Backends (`MoeRunnerBackend` enum)\n**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/moe/utils.py` (lines 86-144)\n\nThe `MoeRunnerBackend` enum defines these backends:\n- `AUTO` -- resolved at runtime based on hardware, model, and quantization\n- `DEEP_GEMM` -- DeepGEMM (NVIDIA, used with DeepEP for DS-V3 style models)\n- `TRITON` -- Triton-based fused MoE kernels (general fallback)\n- `TRITON_KERNELS` -- vllm-style triton_kernels package (GPT-OSS models)\n- `FLASHINFER_TRTLLM` -- FlashInfer TRT-LLM MoE kernels (NVIDIA SM100+)\n- `EXPERIMENTAL_SGL_TRTLLM` -- experimental SGL TRT-LLM variant\n- `FLASHINFER_TRTLLM_ROUTED` -- FlashInfer TRT-LLM routed variant\n- `FLASHINFER_CUTLASS` -- FlashInfer CUTLASS MoE\n- `FLASHINFER_MXFP4` -- FlashInfer MXFP4 MoE\n- `FLASHINFER_CUTEDSL` -- FlashInfer CuteDSL MoE\n- `CUTLASS` -- direct CUTLASS path\n- `MARLIN` -- Marlin quantized MoE\n- `AITER` -- AMD AITER (ROCm-specific, using CK/ASM kernels)\n\n### Runner Instantiation\n**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/moe/moe_runner/runner.py` (lines 27-71)\n\nThe `MoeRunner.__init__` dispatches to the appropriate `MoeRunnerCore`:\n- `TritonRunnerCore` for `TRITON`\n- `TritonKernelsRunnerCore` for `TRITON_KERNELS`\n- `DeepGemmRunnerCore` for `DEEP_GEMM`\n- `AiterRunnerCore` for `AITER` (lazy-imported from `sglang.srt.layers.moe.moe_runner.aiter`)\n- `None` (fused-path only) for `MARLIN`, `FLASHINFER_TRTLLM`, `FLASHINFER_CUTEDSL`, `FLASHINFER_CUTLASS`, `FLASHINFER_MXFP4`, `CUTLASS`\n\n### AUTO Resolution -- Unquantized (BF16) Models\n**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/quantization/unquant.py`, `create_moe_runner` (lines 459-506)\n\nFor unquantized MoE layers, when `moe_runner_backend == AUTO`:\n1. If `use_flashinfer_trtllm_moe` is set -> use the appropriate FlashInfer TRT-LLM variant\n2. If `use_flashinfer_cutlass` -> `FLASHINFER_CUTLASS`\n3. If `use_deep_gemm` -> `DEEP_GEMM`\n4. If `use_triton_kernels` -> `TRITON_KERNELS`\n5. Otherwise -> `TRITON`\n\nA separate `_aiter_runner` is created **alongside** the main runner when:\n- `SGLANG_USE_AITER=1` is set AND on HIP (ROCm)\n- The backend is `AUTO` or explicitly `AITER`\n- The A2A backend supports AITER\n- `intermediate_size_per_partition % 128 == 0` (128-alignment required by AITER CK kernels)\n\nAt forward time (line 587), if `_aiter_runner` is not None, it is used **instead of** the main triton runner. This is the key AMD fallback: on ROCm with AITER enabled, unquantized models prefer AITER but fall back to Triton if the dimensions are not 128-aligned.\n\n### AUTO Resolution -- FP8 Quantized Models\n**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/quantization/fp8.py`, `create_moe_runner` (lines 1859-1887)\n\nFor FP8 MoE, when `moe_runner_backend == AUTO`:\n1. If `is_deepgemm_moe_runner_backend_enabled()` -> `DEEP_GEMM` (requires JIT DeepGEMM + DeepEP/Mooncake/Nixl A2A)\n2. **If `_is_hip` AND (`_use_aiter` OR `_use_hip_int4`) AND the A2A backend supports AITER -> `AITER`** -- this is where AMD/ROCm gets its preferred backend\n3. Otherwise -> `TRITON`\n\nThe static method `is_deepgemm_moe_runner_backend_enabled()` (lines 948-963) checks for NVIDIA DeepGEMM + DeepEP-family backends -- this path is never taken on AMD.\n\n## 2. AMD/ROCm-Specific References in MoE Code\n\n### AITER Runner\n**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/moe/moe_runner/aiter.py`\n\nThis is the AMD-exclusive MoE runner. It wraps the `aiter.fused_moe.fused_moe` function from ROCm's AITER library (Composable Kernel / ASM-based kernels). It supports:\n- Unquantized BF16 (`AiterQuantType.NONE`)\n- FP8 per-token (`AiterQuantType.PER_TOKEN`)\n- FP8 per-block-128x128 (`AiterQuantType.PER_128X128`)\n- MXFP4/W4A4 per-1x32 (`AiterQuantType.PER_1X32`)\n\n### ROCm MoE Utilities\n**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/moe/rocm_moe_utils.py`\n\nContains AMD-specific functions:\n- `rocm_aiter_asm_moe_tkw1` -- wraps AITER's `asm_moe_tkw1` (assembly MoE top-k=1) kernel\n- `rocm_fused_experts_tkw1` -- entry point for FP8 per-channel quantized MoE with `apply_router_weight_on_input`\n- `upscale` / `upscale_mxfp4` -- Triton kernels for dequantizing FP8/MXFP4 dispatched hidden states back to BF16 (used by MoriEP dispatcher on ROCm)\n\n### MXFP8 MoE for AMD gfx95 (CDNA4)\n**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/moe/moe_runner/triton_utils/mxfp8_moe_amd_gfx95.py`\n\nA native MXFP8 MoE implementation for AMD CDNA4 (gfx950, i.e., MI350) using Triton's `tl.dot_scaled` instruction. This provides grouped GEMM with MXFP8 (1x32 block, E8M0 scale) precision. It is specifically invoked from the `TritonRunnerCore.run()` method (in `moe_runner/triton.py`, line 86):\n\n```python\nif quant_info.use_mxfp8 and is_hip() and is_gfx95_supported():\n    from ...triton_utils.mxfp8_moe_amd_gfx95 import fused_experts_mxfp8\n```\n\nThis is a gfx950-specific optimization that would not be used on gfx942 (MI300).\n\n### gfx95 / gfx942 Detection\n**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/utils/common.py` (lines 1015-1034)\n\n- `is_gfx95_supported()` -- returns True if `torch.version.hip` is set and `gcnArchName` contains \"gfx95\" (MI350 / CDNA4)\n- `is_gfx942_supported()` -- returns True for \"gfx942\" (MI300X / MI325X / CDNA3)\n- `_is_shuffle_moe_mxfp4 = is_gfx95_supported()` (in fp8.py, line 118) -- gates MXFP4 shuffle on gfx95\n\n### Configuration File for gfx950 MoE\n**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/moe/moe_runner/triton_utils/configs/minimax_m3_gfx950_mxfp8_compact_moe.json`\n\nA tuning config JSON for the MiniMax-M3 model's MXFP8 MoE on gfx950.\n\n### Key Environment Variables (AMD/ROCm MoE)\n**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/environ.py`\n\n- `SGLANG_USE_AITER` (default: False) -- master switch to enable AITER kernels on ROCm\n- `SGLANG_USE_AITER_FP8_PER_TOKEN` (default: False) -- use per-token FP8 quantization with AITER\n- `SGLANG_USE_AITER_MOE_GU_ITLV` (default: True) -- gate/up weight layout mode (INTERLEAVE vs SEPARATED) for AITER MoE\n- `SGLANG_INT4_WEIGHT` (default: False) -- enable INT4 weight packing on HIP (INT4 MoE weight, FP8 compute)\n\n## 3. How gemma-4-26B Maps to MoE Execution Paths\n\n### Model Architecture\n**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/models/gemma4_causal.py`\n\nThe `Gemma4DecoderLayer` conditionally includes MoE when `config.enable_moe_block == True` (line 606). When MoE is enabled:\n\n1. A `Gemma4Router` (lines 131-186) preprocesses input with RMSNorm + learned scale, then projects to expert logits\n2. A `Gemma4MoE` (lines 189-276) wraps the MoE dispatch, using:\n   - Custom routing: softmax over all experts, then top-k, then renormalize. Per-expert output scales are folded into routing weights\n   - `activation=\"gelu\"` (NOT silu -- this is important for backend compatibility)\n   - `get_moe_impl_class(quant_config)` to determine the MoE implementation (returns `FusedMoE` for standard backends)\n\n### Backend Selection for Gemma4\n**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/arg_groups/overrides.py`, `_gemma4_overrides` (lines 604-630)\n\nThe override handler for Gemma4 (`Gemma4ForConditionalGeneration`, `Gemma4ForCausalLM`, `Gemma4UnifiedForConditionalGeneration`) does:\n- Sets attention backend to `trtllm_mha` on SM100 (Blackwell), `triton` otherwise\n- On SM100 with `modelopt_fp4` quantization: sets `moe_runner_backend = \"flashinfer_trtllm\"`\n- **No Gemma4-specific override for AMD** -- on AMD/ROCm, Gemma4 falls through to the generic auto resolution\n\n### Resolution Path for Gemma4 on AMD/ROCm\n\nSince Gemma4 has no AMD-specific overrides, the `moe_runner_backend` stays `\"auto\"` and resolves as follows:\n\n1. **Unquantized (BF16):** In `UnquantizedFusedMoEMethod.create_moe_runner`, the main runner becomes `TRITON`. If `SGLANG_USE_AITER=1`, a separate `_aiter_runner` is created (if `intermediate_size_per_partition % 128 == 0`). At forward time, the AITER runner is preferred when available.\n\n2. **FP8 quantized:** In `Fp8MoEMethod.create_moe_runner`, on `_is_hip` with `_use_aiter`, the backend resolves directly to `AITER`.\n\n**Important note:** Gemma4 uses `activation=\"gelu\"` for its MoE experts. The AITER runner maps this to `ActivationType.Gelu` (line 100 of `aiter.py`). The native MXFP8 gfx95 path (`mxfp8_moe_amd_gfx95.py`) only supports `activation=\"silu\"` with gated SwiGLU (line 388-391), so it would **not** be used for Gemma4's GELU activation on MI350.\n\n## 4. Fallback Logic Where a Faster Backend is Skipped on AMD\n\n### Fallback 1: DeepGEMM Skipped on AMD\n**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/quantization/fp8.py`, lines 1865-1875\n\nDeepGEMM is only enabled when `is_deepgemm_moe_runner_backend_enabled()` returns True, which requires `deep_gemm_wrapper.ENABLE_JIT_DEEPGEMM` -- an NVIDIA-only capability. On AMD, this check always fails, so DeepGEMM is never selected. The code falls through to AITER (if enabled) or TRITON.\n\n### Fallback 2: FlashInfer TRT-LLM Backends Skipped on AMD\n**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/arg_groups/overrides.py`\n\nThe `flashinfer_trtllm`, `flashinfer_trtllm_routed`, `flashinfer_cutlass`, and `flashinfer_cutedsl` backends are only auto-selected when `is_sm100_supported()` or `is_sm90_supported()` -- NVIDIA-only checks. They are never auto-selected on AMD.\n\n### Fallback 3: AITER CK Dimension Alignment\n**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/quantization/unquant.py`, lines 454-506\n\nThe `_aiter_ck_moe_supported` method (line 454) checks `layer.intermediate_size_per_partition % 128 == 0`. If this fails:\n- When backend is explicitly `AITER` -> raises ValueError\n- When backend is `AUTO` -> logs a warning and falls back to the Triton MoE runner\nThis is the primary \"faster backend skipped on AMD\" pattern: AITER (CK/ASM kernels) is faster but has stricter alignment requirements.\n\n### Fallback 4: GPT-OSS BF16 on ROCm with AITER\n**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/arg_groups/overrides.py`, lines 539-544\n\nFor GPT-OSS models on ROCm with AITER enabled, the override explicitly forces `moe_runner_backend = \"triton\"` with the comment: \"aiter CK kernel doesn't support all GEMM dimensions.\" This is an explicit skip of the faster AITER backend for GPT-OSS BF16 on AMD.\n\n### Fallback 5: BF16 DeepEP Low-Latency on AMD Skipped\n**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/quantization/unquant.py`, lines 329-338\n\nThe DeepGEMM BF16 path with DeepEP low-latency quant config is explicitly skipped when `_is_hip` is True:\n```python\nif (self.use_deep_gemm and layer.w13_weight.dtype == torch.bfloat16\n    and get_moe_a2a_backend().is_deepep()\n    and get_deepep_mode().enable_low_latency()\n    and not _is_npu\n    and not _is_hip  # <-- AMD skipped\n    and hasattr(layer, \"dispatcher\")):\n```\n\n### Fallback 6: FP8 Weight Shuffling on gfx95\n**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/quantization/fp8.py`, lines 632-638\n\nThe `_use_aiter_bpreshuffle_gfx95` flag enables optimized block-preshuffle FP8 linear on CDNA4 (gfx950). When `use_aiter_triton_gemm_w8a8_tuned_gfx950()` returns False for a particular (N, K) shape, the code falls back from AITER to a non-AITER Triton path, with the comment \"drops as use_aiter_triton_gemm_w8a8_tuned_gfx950() expands.\"\n\n## Summary of Key Files\n\n| Purpose | File Path |\n|---------|-----------|\n| MoE backend enum + globals | It supports:\n- Unquantized BF16 (`AiterQuantType.NONE`)\n- FP8 per-token (`AiterQuantType.PER_TOKEN`)\n- FP8 per-block-128x128 (`AiterQuantType.PER_128X128`)\n- MXFP4/W4A4 per-1x32 (`AiterQuantType.PER_1X32`)\n\n### ROCm MoE Utilities\n**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/moe/rocm_moe_utils.py`\n\nContains AMD-specific functions:\n- `rocm_aiter_asm_moe_tkw1` -- wraps AITER's `asm_moe_tkw1` (assembly MoE top-k=1) kernel\n- `rocm_fused_experts_tkw1` -- entry point for FP8 per-channel quantized MoE with `apply_router_weight_on_input`\n- `upscale` / `upscale_mxfp4` -- Triton kernels for dequantizing FP8/MXFP4 dispatched hidden states back to BF16 (used by MoriEP dispatcher on ROCm)\n\n### MXFP8 MoE for AMD gfx95 (CDNA4)\n**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/moe/moe_runner/triton_utils/mxfp8_moe_amd_gfx95.py`\n\nA native MXFP8 MoE implementation for AMD CDNA4 (gfx950, i.e., MI350) using Triton's `tl.dot_scaled` instruction. This provides grouped GEMM with MXFP8 (1x32 block, E8M0 scale) precision. It is specifically invoked from the `TritonRunnerCore.run()` method (in `moe_runner/triton.py`, line 86):\n\n```python\nif quant_info.use_mxfp8 and is_hip() and is_gfx95_supported():\n    from ...triton_utils.mxfp8_moe_amd_gfx95 import fused_experts_mxfp8\n```\n\nThis is a gfx950-specific optimization that would not be used on gfx942 (MI300).\n\n### gfx95 / gfx942 Detection\n**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/utils/common.py` (lines 1015-1034)\n\n- `is_gfx95_supported()` -- returns True if `torch.version.hip` is set and `gcnArchName` contains \"gfx95\" (MI350 / CDNA4)\n- `is_gfx942_supported()` -- returns True for \"gfx942\" (MI300X / MI325X / CDNA3)\n- `_is_shuffle_moe_mxfp4 = is_gfx95_supported()` (in fp8.py, line 118) -- gates MXFP4 shuffle on gfx95\n\n### Configuration File for gfx950 MoE\n**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/moe/moe_runner/triton_utils/configs/minimax_m3_gfx950_mxfp8_compact_moe.json`\n\nA tuning config JSON for the MiniMax-M3 model's MXFP8 MoE on gfx950.\n\n### Key Environment Variables (AMD/ROCm MoE)\n**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/environ.py`\n\n- `SGLANG_USE_AITER` (default: False) -- master switch to enable AITER kernels on ROCm\n- `SGLANG_USE_AITER_FP8_PER_TOKEN` (default: False) -- use per-token FP8 quantization with AITER\n- `SGLANG_USE_AITER_MOE_GU_ITLV` (default: True) -- gate/up weight layout mode (INTERLEAVE vs SEPARATED) for AITER MoE\n- `SGLANG_INT4_WEIGHT` (default: False) -- enable INT4 weight packing on HIP (INT4 MoE weight, FP8 compute)\n\n## 3. How gemma-4-26B Maps to MoE Execution Paths\n\n### Model Architecture\n**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/models/gemma4_causal.py`\n\nThe `Gemma4DecoderLayer` conditionally includes MoE when `config.enable_moe_block == True` (line 606). When MoE is enabled:\n\n1. A `Gemma4Router` (lines 131-186) preprocesses input with RMSNorm + learned scale, then projects to expert logits\n2. A `Gemma4MoE` (lines 189-276) wraps the MoE dispatch, using:\n   - Custom routing: softmax over all experts, then top-k, then renormalize. Per-expert output scales are folded into routing weights\n   - `activation=\"gelu\"` (NOT silu -- this is important for backend compatibility)\n   - `get_moe_impl_class(quant_config)` to determine the MoE implementation (returns `FusedMoE` for standard backends)\n\n### Backend Selection for Gemma4\n**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/arg_groups/overrides.py`, `_gemma4_overrides` (lines 604-630)\n\nThe override handler for Gemma4 (`Gemma4ForConditionalGeneration`, `Gemma4ForCausalLM`, `Gemma4UnifiedForConditionalGeneration`) does:\n- Sets attention backend to `trtllm_mha` on SM100 (Blackwell), `triton` otherwise\n- On SM100 with `modelopt_fp4` quantization: sets `moe_runner_backend = \"flashinfer_trtllm\"`\n- **No Gemma4-specific override for AMD** -- on AMD/ROCm, Gemma4 falls through to the generic auto resolution\n\n### Resolution Path for Gemma4 on AMD/ROCm\n\nSince Gemma4 has no AMD-specific overrides, the `moe_runner_backend` stays `\"auto\"` and resolves as follows:\n\n1. **Unquantized (BF16):** In `UnquantizedFusedMoEMethod.create_moe_runner`, the main runner becomes `TRITON`. If `SGLANG_USE_AITER=1`, a separate `_aiter_runner` is created (if `intermediate_size_per_partition % 128 == 0`). At forward time, the AITER runner is preferred when available.\n\n2. **FP8 quantized:** In `Fp8MoEMethod.create_moe_runner`, on `_is_hip` with `_use_aiter`, the backend resolves directly to `AITER`.\n\n**Important note:** Gemma4 uses `activation=\"gelu\"` for its MoE experts. The AITER runner maps this to `ActivationType.Gelu` (line 100 of `aiter.py`). The native MXFP8 gfx95 path (`mxfp8_moe_amd_gfx95.py`) only supports `activation=\"silu\"` with gated SwiGLU (line 388-391), so it would **not** be used for Gemma4's GELU activation on MI350.\n\n## 4. Fallback Logic Where a Faster Backend is Skipped on AMD\n\n### Fallback 1: DeepGEMM Skipped on AMD\n**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/quantization/fp8.py`, lines 1865-1875\n\nDeepGEMM is only enabled when `is_deepgemm_moe_runner_backend_enabled()` returns True, which requires `deep_gemm_wrapper.ENABLE_JIT_DEEPGEMM` -- an NVIDIA-only capability. On AMD, this check always fails, so DeepGEMM is never selected. The code falls through to AITER (if enabled) or TRITON.\n\n### Fallback 2: FlashInfer TRT-LLM Backends Skipped on AMD\n**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/arg_groups/overrides.py`\n\nThe `flashinfer_trtllm`, `flashinfer_trtllm_routed`, `flashinfer_cutlass`, and `flashinfer_cutedsl` backends are only auto-selected when `is_sm100_supported()` or `is_sm90_supported()` -- NVIDIA-only checks. They are never auto-selected on AMD.\n\n### Fallback 3: AITER CK Dimension Alignment\n**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/quantization/unquant.py`, lines 454-506\n\nThe `_aiter_ck_moe_supported` method (line 454) checks `layer.intermediate_size_per_partition % 128 == 0`. If this fails:\n- When backend is explicitly `AITER` -> raises ValueError\n- When backend is `AUTO` -> logs a warning and falls back to the Triton MoE runner\nThis is the primary \"faster backend skipped on AMD\" pattern: AITER (CK/ASM kernels) is faster but has stricter alignment requirements.\n\n### Fallback 4: GPT-OSS BF16 on ROCm with AITER\n**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/arg_groups/overrides.py`, lines 539-544\n\nFor GPT-OSS models on ROCm with AITER enabled, the override explicitly forces `moe_runner_backend = \"triton\"` with the comment: \"aiter CK kernel doesn't support all GEMM dimensions.\" This is an explicit skip of the faster AITER backend for GPT-OSS BF16 on AMD.\n\n### Fallback 5: BF16 DeepEP Low-Latency on AMD Skipped\n**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/quantization/unquant.py`, lines 329-338\n\nThe DeepGEMM BF16 path with DeepEP low-latency quant config is explicitly skipped when `_is_hip` is True:\n```python\nif (self.use_deep_gemm and layer.w13_weight.dtype == torch.bfloat16\n    and get_moe_a2a_backend().is_deepep()\n    and get_deepep_mode().enable_low_latency()\n    and not _is_npu\n    and not _is_hip  # <-- AMD skipped\n    and hasattr(layer, \"dispatcher\")):\n```\n\n### Fallback 6: FP8 Weight Shuffling on gfx95\n**File:** `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/quantization/fp8.py`, lines 632-638\n\nThe `_use_aiter_bpreshuffle_gfx95` flag enables optimized block-preshuffle FP8 linear on CDNA4 (gfx950). When `use_aiter_triton_gemm_w8a8_tuned_gfx950()` returns False for a particular (N, K) shape, the code falls back from AITER to a non-AITER Triton path, with the comment \"drops as use_aiter_triton_gemm_w8a8_tuned_gfx950() expands.\"\n\n## Summary of Key Files\n\n| Purpose | File Path |\n|---------|-----------|\n| MoE backend enum + globals | configuration are DECLARED here and\nresolved into the flags tier through the ``apply_model_overrides`` gate —\nmodel code never mutates ``ServerArgs``, which stays the pristine user input.\n\nTwo declaration forms, keyed on ``hf_config.architectures[0]``:\n\n- ``MODEL_OVERRIDES``: pure-constant cases — ``arch -> {field: value}``.\n- ``@register_model_override(arch)``: derived cases — a callable\n  ``fn(server_args, hf_config) -> dict`` that faithfully carries today's\n  conditional logic. ``server_args`` is pristine and must be treated\n  read-only: the callable returns declarations, it never writes.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport dataclasses\nimport logging\nfrom typing import Any, Callable, Dict, Iterable, List, Optional, Sequence, Tuple\n\nfrom sglang.srt.arg_groups.arg_utils import resolvable_fields\nfrom sglang.srt.model_executor.cuda_graph_config import Backend\nfrom sglang.srt.runtime_context import resolve_flag_leaf\nfrom
